title: Can you create a general automation to move items up or down to groups within the same board?
excerpt:
I have a board set up with groups which represent the phases that ALL the items need to go through to complete a workflow. 
Is there the ability to change status of an item (regardless of the group it’s within) and make …

posts.author: sorigami (Cory Woods)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have a board set up with groups which represent the phases that ALL the items need to go through to complete a workflow.

Is there the ability to change status of an item (regardless of the group it’s within) and make it move down to the next group/phase?

posts.author: JoseV (Jose )
posts.cookedHtml:
¡Hello Cory! How are you doing?
You can create automations that link each status change to a different group. I show you how it could look like below.

posts.author: sorigami (Cory Woods)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks Jose!

I’m actually curious about the ability to create one automation that pushes items ‘down’ to different groups based on status being complete.

ie:

Group 1:
item a - status=open
item b - status =open
item c - status (open to ready) now item c would move down to group 2

Group 2:
item d - status=open
item e - status =open
item f - status (open to ready) now item f would move down to group 3

**This would remove the need to create an automation for every status and or group name and would apply a general rule for items to move down groups (for process based workflows) when status changes.

posts.author: JoseV (Jose )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Cory! Oh I understand now.

Unfortunately it seems to me that it is not possible with the native tools currently, the only viable option I see is as I said, create a state for each group, and that is the trigger for your workflow.

Captura de Pantalla 2024-02-13 a la(s) 14.58.59781×413 18.9 KB

I hope this is of some use to you and if not, hopefully another partner can provide a workaround to this.

posts.author: sorigami (Cory Woods)
posts.cookedHtml:
Agreed. Your solution does work for flow but not in regards to reducing the amount of automations needed to accomplish said function.

Thanks for your reply
